visual_notes: Dark theme is genuinely polished. Custom SVG characters with per-worker personality (Dave's headphones, Sofia's magnifying glass, Luna's crescent) are charming and render crisply. The vibe-reactive whiteboard doodle (╰(*°▽°*)╯ vs (╯°□°)╯︵ ┻━┻) is a detail that earns its place. Office window gradient for time-of-day is a nice ambient touch. Bloberto's face expressions per vibe are legitimately good character work. Mobile is still cramped — 24px avatars on a 375px screen are barely identifiable.

suggestions.title: stat-dispatch scroll button silently fails on Office tab
suggestions.description: In StatsBar, the 'Active' stat card dispatches a label (e.g. 'skeleton crew') with role='button' that calls document.querySelector('.workers-grid')?.scrollIntoView(). But .workers-grid only exists when the Dashboard tab is active. On the Office tab, the click does nothing — no feedback, no navigation, no error. Fix: check which tab is active and either switch to Dashboard first, or don't render the scroll affordance on the Office tab. Same issue on the Stuck card.

suggestions.title: Root-level useTimeTick causes entire App tree to re-render every 60 seconds
suggestions.description: App() calls useTimeTick(60_000) at line 676 and discards the return value. The stated purpose (keep relative timestamps fresh) is already served by StatsBar's own useTimeTick. Because the root App re-renders every 60s, ALL non-memoized children re-render: WorkerCard, ActivityLog, ActivityLogEntry, StatsBar, and Office. Most have React.memo but their props often change by reference (activityByWorker is a Map reconstructed each time). Remove the useTimeTick call from App(). The only place timestamps actually need to refresh is ActivityLogEntry — pass tick from StatsBar's useTimeTick as a prop, or use a dedicated context.

suggestions.title: Wandering idle characters lose keyboard focus to animation-driven positions
suggestions.description: Idle workers use CSS keyframe animations (char--wander-1 through char--wander-4) to physically move across the office floor. These elements have tabIndex={0} and role='button'. When a keyboard user tabs to them, the browser places focus on the element at its current animated position — which could be anywhere on screen, and changes every few seconds. The focus ring appears at an unpredictable location. Fix: for keyboard-only navigation, pause or suppress the wander animation on :focus-within, or provide a static accessible alternative (a skip-to-worker list in the sr-only region). At minimum, add CSS: .char:focus-within { animation-play-state: paused; }

suggestions.title: Error boundary recovery: no actionable CTA, just 'refresh the page'
suggestions.description: The ErrorBoundary fallback renders plain text: 'Something went wrong — refresh the page'. On mobile, users may not know how to hard-refresh. The fallback has no styling, no button, and no recovery path. Fix: add a styled <button onClick={() => window.location.reload()}>Refresh</button> inside the fallback, and wrap it in a centered container matching the app's design tokens. Also add componentDidCatch logging so errors aren't silently swallowed in production.

suggestions.title: formatDuration and formatIdleDuration are near-duplicate utility functions
suggestions.description: formatDuration (App.jsx ~L261) returns 'Working for Xh Ym'. formatIdleDuration (Office.jsx ~L470) returns 'Xh Ym'. These share identical arithmetic, differ only in the 'Working for' prefix and the <1m fallback text. Extract a single getHumanDuration(ms) into utils/time.js. Callers add the prefix. Also getRelativeTime and formatDuration both compute hours/mins from a timestamp diff — consolidate the date math. The current sprawl means any fix to the duration logic needs to happen in 4 places.

suggestions.title: ConferenceTable aria-label is static and doesn't reflect meeting state
suggestions.description: ConferenceTable always has aria-label='Conference table' regardless of state. When a meeting is active (meetingWorkers.length >= 2), a screen reader user learns nothing about who is present or that a huddle is occurring. When standup is active, same problem. Fix: dynamically build the aria-label: e.g. 'Conference table — huddle in progress with Maya and Dave' or 'Conference table — standup active'. The existing conf-meeting-badge text ('Huddle · 3m') already has this information; surface it to the accessible tree.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)') called on every click in Character
suggestions.description: In the Character component's handleClick, window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches is called inline on every single click event. This creates a new MediaQueryList object each time (browser GC pressure, minor but pointless). Additionally, the pointer-coarse check is repeated in App.jsx for the polling-paused button label. Fix: memoize at module level: const IS_TOUCH = window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches — or use a small useMediaQuery hook that subscribes to the change event for correctness (user can plug in a mouse after load).

suggestions.title: Ghost characters (OOO roster members) are hover-interactive but click-dead
suggestions.description: Ghost characters (roster members not currently active) show a 'OOO · Last seen: Xm ago' bubble on hover, suggesting they're interactive. But they have no onClick handler and tabIndex={-1}. A user on mobile (who triggers hover state on first tap) sees the bubble, taps again expecting something to happen — nothing. Fix: either make ghost characters fully non-interactive (remove ghostBubble state, set cursor:default) or make them clickable and navigate to their filtered activity log in the Dashboard. Don't leave affordances dangling.

suggestions.title: Add idleness countdown notification before polling auto-pauses
suggestions.description: IDLE_TIMEOUT is 10 minutes of no mouse/keyboard/touch activity before polling pauses. Users get zero warning before this happens. A manager walks away for 8 minutes, comes back, and sees stale data with a 'PAUSED' pill they may not notice. Fix: show a subtle banner or badge at ~2 minutes before pause: '⏱ Auto-refresh pauses in 2m due to inactivity'. This also helps users understand WHY data went stale. The existing 15-second interval check in the polling-paused effect is the right place to add this threshold check.

suggestions.title: Activity log needs load-more or virtualization for replay value
suggestions.description: The activity log is hard-capped at displaying 20 entries (displayed = [...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20)). The server may return more; they're fetched but discarded. For a team manager doing a retrospective, seeing 'Maya shipped 3 tasks today' requires scrolling through 20+ entries — which is impossible. Fix: add a 'Load more' button that increments the displayed count by 20. Alternatively, if the full array is already in memory (activityLog state), just paginate client-side. Also remove the server-side cap — let the client decide what to show.

suggestions.title: No export or share functionality for activity log data
suggestions.description: The activity log has type filters and worker filters — users are clearly expected to investigate specific worker histories. But there's no way to share a filtered view (no URL state for filters) and no way to export (CSV, clipboard). For a QA engineer or manager writing a post-mortem, copying 20 entries manually is a friction point. Fix: (1) serialize activityFilter and workerFilter to URL query params so filtered views are shareable; (2) add a small 'Copy log' button that puts the filtered entries on the clipboard as formatted text. Both are small wins.

suggestions.title: focusedWorker highlight clears on a timer rather than on user action
suggestions.description: handleWorkerClick sets focusedWorker and schedules a 3-second timeout to clear it. If the user clicks a worker card, tabs to the dashboard, and the card scrollIntoView runs, the 3s glow highlight disappears automatically — even if the user is still reading it. On slower connections, the data may not have loaded by the time the highlight vanishes. Fix: clear focusedWorker on any user interaction with the grid (onMouseMove, onKeyDown on the grid container) rather than on a fixed 3-second timer. The user should control when the focus state dismisses.

suggestions.title: vibe-history localStorage parse failure silently swallowed
suggestions.description: In the vibeHistory useEffect, localStorage.getItem and JSON.parse are wrapped in try/catch with an empty catch block (/* ignore */). If localStorage is full, corrupt, or in private-browsing mode, vibe history silently disappears with no fallback. This means the VibeSparkline shows nothing with no indication why. Fix: catch the error and set vibeHistory to []. Also add a defensive check: if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.localStorage) before the try. The /* ignore */ pattern is a code smell — at minimum add a console.warn so developers can detect storage issues during QA.

suggestions.title: Stuck worker alert (>2h) has no escalation path or acknowledgement
suggestions.description: The stat-card--stuck card correctly flags workers stuck for over 2 hours. But clicking it only scrolls to the worker card. There's no way to acknowledge the alert ('yes, I know Dave has been stuck, stop flashing') and no escalation — no ping, no external notification. For a real team manager, 2h stuck with no acknowledgement is exactly the scenario that needs an alert. Fix: add an 'Ack' button that stores the acknowledged timestamp in localStorage, suppresses the stuck badge for that worker for 1h, and shows a small 'acknowledged X ago' tag. This gives the UI memory.
